tasks.title: Retrieve relevant documents and policies about Booking.com no-show and cancellation disputes
Search for recent cases and guidelines on Booking.com no-show complaints and guest cancellations
Analyze gathered information to identify steps for complaint triage and resolution
Develop a structured triage plan including verification, communication, and escalation procedures
Summarize the triage plan and prepare it for implementation

rag_output.summary:
- Support must verify the timeline when a property marks a reservation as no-show after the guest claims cancellation; any timeline conflicts should be escalated [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show, the charge should be treated as potentially refundable, requiring collection of cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner details [2].  
- Determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or property); if the property charged directly, gather payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before proceeding [3].  

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] refunds_policy.md, [3] payment_disputes.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: When the property marked the reservation as a no-show after the guest claims to have cancelled, support must verify the timeline before declining the request. Timeline conflicts should be escalated.

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the guest cancelled inside the free-cancellation window and the booking was not later marked as a no-show, support should treat the charge as potentially refundable and gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

web_output.summary: No public Booking.com evidence was found from the configured web sources.

action_plan:
- Verify the exact timeline of the guest’s cancellation and when the property marked the booking as no-show; escalate if there are conflicting timestamps or unclear sequence of events.  
- Confirm if the cancellation occurred within the free-cancellation window and whether the booking was marked no-show despite this; if so, collect the cancellation timestamp, property cancellation policy, and payment ownership details to assess refund eligibility.  
- Determine who processed the payment—Booking.com or the property; if the property charged directly, request the payment receipt, message trail with the property, and cancellation timeline before making a refund decision.
next_steps:
1. Contact the guest to obtain the exact date and time they cancelled the booking and request any supporting evidence (e.g., cancellation confirmation email).  
2. Retrieve the property’s no-show marking timestamp and cancellation policy to compare against the guest’s cancellation timeline.  
3. Verify payment processing details to identify whether Booking.com or the property charged the guest; if the property charged directly, request the payment receipt and message history with the property.  
4. Escalate the case to the legal or disputes team if there are conflicting timelines, unclear payment ownership, or if the cancellation falls within the free-cancellation window but the booking was still marked no-show.
final_answer:
Issue:  
A property marked a guest’s booking as a no-show, but the guest claims they cancelled the reservation. There is a dispute regarding the timeline and validity of the cancellation versus the no-show designation.

Internal policy:  
- Support must verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation and the property’s no-show marking; any conflicting or unclear timelines require escalation.  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was still marked no-show, the charge may be refundable. In this case, collect the cancellation timestamp, property cancellation policy, and payment ownership details.  
- Determine who collected the payment—Booking.com or the property. If the property charged directly, gather the payment receipt, message trail with the property, and cancellation timeline before proceeding.

Public policy:  
No publicly available Booking.com policy or evidence addresses this specific scenario.

Recommended action:  
- Contact the guest to obtain the exact cancellation date/time and any supporting evidence such as cancellation confirmation emails.  
- Retrieve the property’s no-show marking timestamp and cancellation policy to compare with the guest’s cancellation timeline.  
- Verify payment processing details to identify whether Booking.com or the property charged the guest. If the property charged directly, request the payment receipt and message history with the property.  
- If there are timeline conflicts, unclear payment ownership, or if the cancellation was within the free-cancellation window but the booking was marked no-show, escalate the case to the legal or disputes team for further review.

Next steps:  
1. Reach out to the guest for cancellation details and evidence.  
2. Obtain the property’s no-show marking timestamp and cancellation policy.  
3. Confirm payment ownership and collect relevant payment documentation if needed.  
4. Escalate to the appropriate internal team if discrepancies or refund eligibility concerns arise.
